sanskrit:
ॐ तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः।
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा॥
transliteration:
oṃ tat sad iti nirdeśo brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛtaḥ |
brāhmaṇās tena vedāś ca yajñāś ca vihitāḥ purā ||
literal_meaning: The syllables oṃ tat sat are the three-fold designation of the Absolute; by them brāhmaṇas, Vedas and sacrifices were instituted of old.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a mantra-anchor that turns any act into a psychospiritual procedure: when intention, action and meaning are tagged with oṃ-tat-sat they are re-coded from ego-driven to Self-referential, reducing cognitive dissonance and increasing felt coherence.
psychological_state: Post-decision cognitive stabilization—Arjuna has chosen to fight, now needs a mental device to keep actions from fragmenting his self-concept.
trigger_scenario: Before hitting 'send' on a high-stakes email you silently tag it 'om tat sat', shifting focus from personal win to service of a larger mission, calming amygdala arousal.
anchor_text: Label every thought, word, and deed with the quiet pulse: oṃ-tat-sat.
